Name: Restricted Administrative/Onomastic Script
Claim: The inscriptions encode restricted names, titles, institutions, commodities, or authority formulae rather than full prose.
MainSupportingEvidence: formula_restriction=0.898 x 1.30; context_strength=0.890 x 1.20; prime_entity_strength=0.654 x 1.00; classifier_strength=0.517 x 0.90; suffixing_strength=0.734 x 0.70
MainObjection: A large unresolved sequence class remains and may hide multiple mechanisms.
DecisiveNextTests: Prime-entity fillers must correlate with object, site, emblem, or administrative context. | Terminal and classifier signs must remain functionally stable across sites. | Longer prose-like syntax should remain rare.

Name: Multilingual Administrative Ecology
Claim: The system is a shared administrative script with possible language-local names or titles at different sites.
MainSupportingEvidence: formula_restriction=0.898 x 1.00; regional_strength=0.733 x 1.20; context_strength=0.890 x 1.10; prime_entity_strength=0.654 x 0.80; classifier_strength=0.517 x 0.80
MainObjection: A large unresolved sequence class remains and may hide multiple mechanisms.
DecisiveNextTests: Regional/site distributions should explain units better than one global phonetic inventory. | A core of signs should remain stable while some fillers show site-specific behavior. | Image/context validation should reveal local administrative practices.

Name: Primarily Non-Linguistic Emblem System
Claim: The signs are mostly emblematic or administrative symbols with little direct language encoding.
MainSupportingEvidence: context_strength=0.890 x 1.30; icon_strength=0.794 x 1.00; formula_restriction=0.898 x 0.90; classifier_strength=0.517 x 0.60; not prime_entity_strength=0.346 x 0.20
MainObjection: Structured prime-entity slots and phonetic-ready minimal pairs are too strong for a purely emblematic account.
DecisiveNextTests: Context associations should dominate over internal grammatical structure. | Phonetic anchors should fail to generalize across artifacts. | Minimal pairs should behave as graphic variants rather than linguistic contrasts.

Name: Dravidian-Compatible Linguistic Layer
Claim: Some signs encode a Dravidian or para-Dravidian linguistic layer, probably through suffixing formulae and names/titles.
MainSupportingEvidence: suffixing_strength=0.734 x 1.30; prime_entity_strength=0.654 x 1.00; phonetic_readiness_strength=0.583 x 0.90; formula_restriction=0.898 x 0.60; context_strength=0.890 x 0.40
MainObjection: No sign has a validated phonetic value yet; family assignment remains below decipherment threshold.
DecisiveNextTests: Suffix-like terminal signs should attach productively to entity fillers. | A proposed Dravidian morpheme must predict all structural occurrences, not just one sign. | Proper-name or title readings must survive cross-site minimal alternations.

Name: Logo-Syllabic or Logo-Phonetic Writing
Claim: The system combines semantic signs with phonetic or syllabic complements.
MainSupportingEvidence: phonetic_readiness_strength=0.583 x 1.25; minimal_anchor_strength=0.775 x 1.00; prime_entity_strength=0.654 x 0.80; classifier_strength=0.517 x 0.60; suffixing_strength=0.734 x 0.60
MainObjection: Minimal-pair evidence exists, but no phonetic complement has been proven.
DecisiveNextTests: Minimal pairs should isolate recurring sound-bearing contrasts. | Sign variables should appear in both semantic and phonetic roles. | Allograph compression should preserve phonetic or morphemic structure.

Name: Indo-Iranian/Indo-Aryan Linguistic Layer
Claim: Some signs encode an early Indo-Iranian or Indo-Aryan linguistic layer, probably in names, titles, or ritual/administrative terms.
MainSupportingEvidence: prime_entity_strength=0.654 x 0.90; phonetic_readiness_strength=0.583 x 1.00; suffixing_strength=0.734 x 0.50; context_strength=0.890 x 0.50; formula_restriction=0.898 x 0.40
MainObjection: No sign has a validated phonetic value yet; family assignment remains below decipherment threshold.
DecisiveNextTests: Any reading must be chronology-sensitive and avoid later Sanskrit back-projection. | Inflectional or title morphology must explain terminal signs and minimal pairs. | A proposed name/title must recur in compatible semantic contexts.

Name: Munda/Para-Munda Linguistic Layer
Claim: Some signs encode a Munda-like or para-Munda linguistic layer, potentially with prefixing or mixed morphology.
MainSupportingEvidence: classifier_strength=0.517 x 0.80; regional_strength=0.733 x 0.70; phonetic_readiness_strength=0.583 x 0.80; suffixing_strength=0.734 x 0.40; context_strength=0.890 x 0.40
MainObjection: No sign has a validated phonetic value yet; family assignment remains below decipherment threshold.
DecisiveNextTests: Prefix-like or classifier-like behavior should be stronger than suffix-only analysis. | Regional distributions should fit plausible eastern or contact-zone contexts. | Readings must explain both initial and terminal alternations.
